Shephard Unmanned

Loitering munitions mature as one-way attack drones proliferate

Shephard News Team · 21 June 2026

Loitering munitions, also called kamikaze drones or one-way attack UAVs, are maturing rapidly as multiple manufacturers field new systems. Recent demonstrations have shown improved range, autonomy and warhead options across the loitering munition category.

Systems such as the SkyStriker and Nagastra have been tested and inducted in various forms, while developers pursue swarming and counter-UAS resilience. The technology is moving from prototype toward operational use, with several programmes at the test stage.

Observers expect loitering munitions to become a standard component of tactical UAV and ISR portfolios through the late 2020s.
